$comment: D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-MULTI-DECLARATOR (C 6.7, 2026-07-18): a MULTI-declarator `extern` declaration — one `extern` head binding N declarators, each with its own pointer/array suffix — at BOTH file scope (`extern int fa, fb;` + `extern int *fp, farr[2];`) AND block scope (`extern int bc, bd;`). The legacy single-declarator externDecl P0009'd on the comma (sqlite test1.c:9341). externDecl is now the extern twin of topLevelDecl (typeRefAllowingStruct + initDeclaratorList); Pass-1 mints N nonDefiningDeclaration symbols, CST->HIR emits N ExternGlobal nodes. All externs are defined in def.c; the LK11 merge binds each import to its sibling-TU definition. fa=5 fb=7 *fp=3 farr[0]=4 farr[1]=6 bc=8 bd=9 -> 42. Value-divergent: a shared-head star mis-typing farr as a pointer, or a mis-scoped block extern, never yields 42. The release arm witnesses the optimizer over the new declarator-list shape. Cross-CU manifest mirrors block_scope_extern / extern_incomplete_array_crosscu (pe + elf run targets; macho 2-TU underscore-mangled cross-CU extern matching stays compile-only, no local run).
